code_dossier.name: HasPermissionResponse.java
code_dossier.description: A Data Transfer Object (DTO) that encapsulates the response for permission checking operations, extending the base response structure to include a boolean flag indicating whether a user has a specific permission.

data.detailed_description:
HasPermissionResponse is a lightweight Data Transfer Object (DTO) class designed to communicate permission check results in the account management API module. 

The class extends BaseResponse, which provides standardized response fields including a ToBCodeEnum code and a message string. This inheritance pattern ensures consistent API response structures across the system.

The class leverages Lombok annotations for boilerplate reduction:
- @Data: Automatically generates getters, setters, toString(), equals(), and hashCode() methods
- @EqualsAndHashCode(callSuper = true): Ensures parent class fields are included in equality comparisons

The core business logic this DTO supports is permission verification - a critical security function in account management systems. When a service checks whether a user has a specific permission, this response object carries both the result (hasPermission boolean) and standard response metadata (code, message) back to the caller.

The design follows the API response envelope pattern common in enterprise Java applications, where responses contain both business data and metadata about the operation's success/failure status.
responsibilities: Encapsulate permission check results in a standardized response format
Extend base response structure with permission-specific data (hasPermission flag)
Provide type-safe data transfer between service layer and API consumers
Maintain consistency with system-wide API response conventions through BaseResponse inheritance

interfaces.name: HasPermissionResponse
interfaces.description: DTO class for permission check response, extending BaseResponse with a hasPermission boolean field. Lombok generates all accessor methods, constructors, and utility methods.

interfaces.name: hasPermission
interfaces.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the queried permission is granted. Getter (isHasPermission/getHasPermission) and setter are auto-generated by Lombok @Data annotation.
